page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5ne.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The system is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and featuring a baseline network bandwidth of 7 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core, fall into the lower 25th percentile, reflecting the older generation of the Intel Xeon 8163 processor. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, with stress-ng multi-core, Passmark CPU, and Geekbench multi-core scores all placing in the middle 50th percentile. Memory bandwidth benchmarks for both small and large blocks also demonstrate average performance, though the Passmark memory score is positioned in the bottom 25th percentile.

Given its balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio and average multi-threaded performance, this instance is suited for general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, Redis database operations, and moderate multi-threaded application hosting. It can handle light LLM inference tasks for small models, though performance drops significantly on larger models. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core allocation provides predictable performance for steady-state workloads, making it a stable choice for enterprise applications that do not require high single-threaded burst capabilities or local NVMe storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5ne.4xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 7 Gbps without local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show a contrast between weak single-core capabilities and average multi-core scaling, with stress-ng multi-core, Passmark CPU, and static web serving throughput all scoring in the middle 50th percentile. This instance is best suited for general-purpose enterprise workloads, including static web serving, Redis key-value stores, and moderate multi-threaded applications that benefit from dedicated CPU resources but do not demand high single-threaded performance or local SSD storage.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5ne.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 7 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering balanced multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5ne.4xlarge features 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores and 64 GB RAM, offering balanced general-purpose performance.
tagline: A dedicated multi-core virtual server offering balanced memory and compute resources for steady-state enterprise workloads and web serving.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio
7 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
Average multi-core performance suitable for static web serving and Redis
Well-suited for general-purpose enterprise applications and moderate multi-threaded workloads
